chapter_summary: Chapter 6 is full of important lessons about how to live wisely. It warns us not to make promises we cannot keep, and it points us to the little ant who works hard without anyone telling her to. It describes the kinds of things that lead to trouble — pride, lying, and hurting others — and lists seven things God hates. Then it urges us to hold on tight to our parents' teaching, because their wisdom is like a lamp that lights our path. The chapter ends with a reminder that wrong choices — especially ones that hurt other people — always bring serious consequences.
main_lesson: God wants us to work hard and make wise choices, just like the busy ant.

memory_verse.kjv: Go to the ant, thou sluggard; consider her ways, and be wise:
memory_verse.little_bible: Look at the ant! She works hard without anyone telling her to. God says: be wise and work like that!

parent_guide: Proverbs 6 covers four distinct sections: (1) The danger of surety — making pledges for others (vv.1-5). (2) The lesson of the ant — diligence vs. laziness (vv.6-11). (3) The portrait of a troublemaker (vv.12-15). (4) Seven things God hates (vv.16-19). (5) The value of parental teaching (vv.20-23). (6) Warnings about relationships that dishonour God (vv.24-35). For children, the accessible core is the ant passage and the seven things God hates. Verses 24-35 address adultery; in this children's edition they are framed around the principle that wrong choices and wrong friendships have real consequences. Parents may use these verses at an age-appropriate level to discuss boundaries, loyalty, and the importance of staying close to what is right.
application_for_children: Watch an ant this week — outside, in a video, or in a book. Notice how it carries things bigger than itself and never gives up. Ask yourself: where in my life can I work harder and not give up? Then do that thing today.

verses.kjv: My son, if thou be surety for thy friend, if thou hast stricken thy hand with a stranger,
verses.little_bible: My child, be very careful when you make a big promise for someone else — especially a promise that puts you in a hard spot.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Be careful making big promises for others.
verses.meaning: Making financial guarantees for others can trap us in obligations we cannot fulfil. Wisdom means thinking carefully before we pledge our word.
verses.memory_phrase: Think carefully before you promise.
verses.prayer: God, help me think before I make a promise so I can always keep my word.
verses.discussion_question: What happens when you make a promise you cannot keep?
verses.family_discussion: Why is it important to only promise things we are sure we can do? How do we handle it when we realise we made a promise that was too big?
verses.do_it_today: Think of one promise you have made. Check: can you really keep it? If not, go and talk to the person kindly.
verses.illustration_prompt: Two children shaking hands on a playground, with one looking a little worried — a moment of making a promise, warm watercolour style

verses.kjv: Thou art snared with the words of thy mouth, thou art taken with the words of thy mouth.
verses.little_bible: Your own words can trap you — like a net that catches you when you have promised something too big.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Careless words can trap you like a net.
verses.meaning: Hasty words create obligations we may deeply regret. Our speech has power to bind us, for good or harm.
verses.memory_phrase: My words have power.
verses.prayer: God, remind me that my words are powerful — help me speak only what I truly mean.
verses.discussion_question: Can you think of a time words got someone into trouble?
verses.family_discussion: How do we cultivate a habit of saying only what we mean in our family?
verses.do_it_today: Before you say 'I promise' today, pause and ask yourself: 'Do I really mean this?'
verses.illustration_prompt: A child looking wide-eyed at a net made of tangled golden words floating around them, watercolour style

verses.kjv: Do this now, my son, and deliver thyself, when thou art come into the hand of thy friend; go, humble thyself, and make sure thy friend.
verses.little_bible: If you have made a promise that is too big, do not wait — go humbly to the person and try to make it right as quickly as you can.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Made a big mistake? Go fix it right away.
verses.meaning: When we realise we have overcommitted, the wise response is humble, immediate action — not delay or pride.
verses.memory_phrase: Fix it quickly and humbly.
verses.prayer: God, give me the courage to go and make things right when I have made a mistake.
verses.discussion_question: Why is it hard to say sorry or ask for help when we have made a mistake?
verses.family_discussion: How does our family handle it when someone needs to undo a wrong? Is it safe to admit mistakes here?
verses.do_it_today: Is there something you need to fix or say sorry for? Do it today — do not wait.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child walking humbly toward another child with hands open, ready to make things right, soft warm light, watercolour style

verses.kjv: Give not sleep to thine eyes, nor slumber to thine eyelids.
verses.little_bible: Do not rest until you have made it right — keep going until the problem is solved.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Do not rest until you fix the problem.
verses.meaning: Urgency matters when it comes to repairing broken commitments. Procrastination only deepens the problem.
verses.memory_phrase: Do not delay — act now.
verses.prayer: God, help me not put off the things I need to do. Give me energy to act today.
verses.discussion_question: What is something you have been putting off that you should do?
verses.family_discussion: How does delaying hard tasks make them harder? Tell about a time acting quickly made things better.
verses.do_it_today: Pick one thing you have been putting off. Do the first small step right now.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child wide awake and determined at their desk, a glowing candle nearby, focused on finishing something important, watercolour style

verses.kjv: Deliver thyself as a roe from the hand of the hunter, and as a bird from the hand of the fowler.
verses.little_bible: Get yourself free from the problem — like a deer that leaps away from the hunter, or a bird that escapes from the trap!
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Escape from trouble like a bird from a trap!
verses.meaning: God wants us to pursue freedom from unwise commitments with the same urgency and energy a trapped animal uses to escape danger.
verses.memory_phrase: Escape trouble quickly and freely.
verses.prayer: God, help me be quick to get away from things that are not good for me.
verses.discussion_question: What do you do when you find yourself in a situation you should not be in?
verses.family_discussion: How can we help each other recognise when we are in a trap and need to get free?
verses.do_it_today: Ask a parent: 'Is there anything I am doing that I should get away from?' Listen carefully.
verses.illustration_prompt: A small bird bursting free from an open cage into bright sky, feathers catching light, joyful watercolour style

verses.kjv: Go to the ant, thou sluggard; consider her ways, and be wise:
verses.little_bible: Look at the ant, you lazy one! Watch how she lives — she will teach you to be wise.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Watch the ant. She teaches us to be wise!
verses.meaning: God points us to creation as a teacher. The ant models diligence, foresight, and self-discipline — all virtues God prizes.
verses.memory_phrase: Watch the ant and be wise.
verses.prayer: God, help me learn from the little ant and be a hard, wise worker.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever watched an ant? What did you notice about how hard it works?
verses.family_discussion: What habits of hard work do we want to build in our family? Who is a role model for diligence for us?
verses.do_it_today: Find an ant outside or look one up in a book. Watch it for two minutes and thank God for the lesson it teaches.
verses.illustration_prompt: A tiny ant carrying a large crumb across a sunlit path, determined and strong, child watching in wonder nearby, warm watercolour style

verses.kjv: Which having no guide, overseer, or ruler,
verses.little_bible: The ant has no boss, no teacher standing over her, and no one telling her what to do every single moment.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The ant works with no one watching her.
verses.meaning: True diligence is internal — it comes from character, not from being watched. The ant teaches self-motivation rooted in purpose.
verses.memory_phrase: Work hard even without a watcher.
verses.prayer: God, help me work hard even when no one is checking on me.
verses.discussion_question: Do you work just as hard when no one is watching as when someone is watching?
verses.family_discussion: How do we build the kind of character that does the right thing when no one sees? What does that look like in our home?
verses.do_it_today: Do one chore or kind act today without being asked — just because it is good to do.
verses.illustration_prompt: A cheerful ant working alone in a tunnel, with no one around, carrying a seed with a small smile, glowing underground scene, watercolour style

verses.kjv: Provideth her meat in the summer, and gathereth her food in the harvest.
verses.little_bible: She gathers food in the summer and harvest time — she plans ahead and stores up what she will need later.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: She gathers food now to have enough later.
verses.meaning: The ant is a model of wise foresight — working in seasons of plenty to prepare for times of need.
verses.memory_phrase: Plan ahead and store up good things.
verses.prayer: God, help me work now so that I am ready for what comes next.
verses.discussion_question: Why is it important to prepare now instead of waiting until you really need something?
verses.family_discussion: How does our family prepare for things ahead of time? What habits help us be ready?
verses.do_it_today: Do something today to be ready for tomorrow — pack your bag, finish your work, or help prepare a meal.
verses.illustration_prompt: A line of ants carrying seeds and crumbs into a cosy underground storehouse, busy and joyful, warm watercolour scene

verses.kjv: How long wilt thou sleep, O sluggard? when wilt thou arise out of thy sleep?
verses.little_bible: How long will you keep sleeping? When will you finally get up and get to work?
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Wake up! Time to get to work!
verses.meaning: The lazy person keeps delaying. God's question is urgent: how long will you waste what you have been given?
verses.memory_phrase: Wake up and get to work!
verses.prayer: God, help me not be lazy. Help me wake up ready to use the day You have given me.
verses.discussion_question: What is the difference between resting when you need to and being lazy?
verses.family_discussion: How do we balance rest and hard work in our family? What are signs we are being lazy rather than resting?
verses.do_it_today: Get up a little earlier tomorrow and use that extra time to do something good.
verses.illustration_prompt: A sleepy child slowly waking up in a sunlit bedroom, a rooster outside the window crowing, warm morning light, watercolour style

verses.kjv: Yet a little sleep, a little slumber, a little folding of the hands to sleep:
verses.little_bible: The lazy person says, 'Just a little more sleep — just a little more rest' — and keeps on putting off what needs to be done.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Just a little more sleep — and more time slips away.
verses.meaning: Small delays add up to big losses. The habit of 'just a little more' robs us of time, opportunity, and fruitfulness.
verses.memory_phrase: Do not put off what can be done now.
verses.prayer: God, help me stop making excuses. Help me get up and do what I need to do today.
verses.discussion_question: What do you say to yourself when you do not want to do something? Is it usually true?
verses.family_discussion: What small delays have we noticed in our family that add up to bigger problems? How can we help each other?
verses.do_it_today: Notice when you are tempted to say 'just a little later' about something good. Do it now instead.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child curled up under blankets with an alarm clock going off on the nightstand, soft morning light coming through the curtains, watercolour style

verses.kjv: So shall thy poverty come as one that travelleth, and thy want as an armed man.
verses.little_bible: If you keep putting off your work, trouble will sneak up on you — and by the time you notice, it will be hard to push back.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Laziness leads to real trouble, quickly.
verses.meaning: The consequences of laziness are not slow — they arrive suddenly and forcefully, like a robber who cannot be easily stopped.
verses.memory_phrase: Laziness always brings trouble.
verses.prayer: God, help me not be surprised by trouble I could have avoided by working wisely.
verses.discussion_question: Can you think of something that went wrong because someone — or you — waited too long?
verses.family_discussion: What areas of life require steady, faithful work even when we do not see results right away?
verses.do_it_today: Think of one area where you have been putting in steady work. Thank God for it and keep going.
verses.illustration_prompt: A messy garden gone to weeds because it was left alone too long, a sad child looking at it, learning a lesson, soft watercolour tones

verses.kjv: A naughty person, a wicked man, walketh with a froward mouth.
verses.little_bible: A troublemaker — someone who lives to cause harm — always has twisted, unkind words coming out of their mouth.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: A troublemaker always speaks unkind, twisted words.
verses.meaning: Character reveals itself through speech. A corrupt heart produces corrupt words — the mouth is a window to what is inside.
verses.memory_phrase: Unkind words show a troubled heart.
verses.prayer: God, fill my heart with good things so that good words come out of my mouth.
verses.discussion_question: What kinds of words tell you that a person has a kind heart? What kinds tell you they have a mean heart?
verses.family_discussion: How can we tell the difference between someone who has made a mistake and someone who is truly living as a troublemaker?
verses.do_it_today: Pay attention to your words today. After each conversation, ask: were those words kind, true, and helpful?
verses.illustration_prompt: A stormy cloud hanging over a scowling figure, with jagged dark words swirling around, contrasted with a bright child holding warm golden words, watercolour style

verses.kjv: He winketh with his eyes, he speaketh with his feet, he teacheth with his fingers;
verses.little_bible: He sends sneaky signals with his eyes, his feet, and his fingers — making secret plans to do wrong.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: He sends sneaky signals to plan bad things.
verses.meaning: A troublemaker communicates deceptively — using body language and secret signals to scheme. This is the opposite of honest, open communication.
verses.memory_phrase: Be open and honest, not sneaky.
verses.prayer: God, help me be honest and open — no sneaky plans or secret signals.
verses.discussion_question: Why do people use sneaky signals instead of speaking plainly? What does that tell us about what they are planning?
verses.family_discussion: How does our family build a culture where we say what we mean openly and honestly?
verses.do_it_today: Practise being direct and kind today — say what you really mean clearly and honestly to someone you care about.
verses.illustration_prompt: A shadowy figure using exaggerated winks and sneaky hand gestures, contrasted with a bright honest child speaking clearly, watercolour illustration

verses.kjv: Frowardness is in his heart, he deviseth mischief continually; he soweth discord.
verses.little_bible: His heart is full of crooked thoughts. He is always planning trouble, and he loves to start fights between people.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: He plans trouble and loves starting fights.
verses.meaning: The troublemaker's defining trait is divisiveness — a constant inward drive to scheme and break relationships apart.
verses.memory_phrase: A wise person builds peace, not fights.
verses.prayer: God, give me a heart that builds people up and brings peace, not one that stirs up trouble.
verses.discussion_question: What is the difference between a peacemaker and a troublemaker?
verses.family_discussion: How do we respond when someone tries to stir up trouble between us and our friends or family?
verses.do_it_today: Be a peacemaker today — if there is a disagreement around you, try to help bring calm and kindness.
verses.illustration_prompt: Two groups of friends looking upset and apart, with a sneaky figure between them, while a kind child steps in with open arms to bring peace, watercolour style

verses.kjv: Therefore shall his calamity come suddenly; suddenly shall he be broken without remedy.
verses.little_bible: And because of all his trouble-making, disaster will come crashing down on him suddenly — and there will be no way to fix it.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Trouble always catches up with a troublemaker.
verses.meaning: God is just. Those who consistently sow harm will reap a sudden, irreversible reckoning — a sobering warning about the fruit of wickedness.
verses.memory_phrase: Wrong choices bring real consequences.
verses.prayer: God, help me remember that every choice has a result — and help me choose what is right.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think trouble always catches up with a troublemaker in the end?
verses.family_discussion: How does knowing that God is just — that wrong always has consequences — shape the choices we make?
verses.do_it_today: Think of one choice you make regularly. Ask: where will this choice lead me in five years?
verses.illustration_prompt: A path that looked smooth but leads to a sudden steep cliff edge, a figure stopping in shock, God's gentle presence watching over, watercolour style

verses.kjv: These six things doth the LORD hate: yea, seven are an abomination unto him:
verses.little_bible: There are six — no, seven! — things that God truly hates. These are things that are deeply wrong and harmful.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: God hates seven harmful things. Listen carefully!
verses.meaning: The numerical pattern (six, then seven) is a literary device to create anticipation. It signals that what follows is especially important.
verses.memory_phrase: God hates what is truly harmful.
verses.prayer: God, help me learn what You hate so I can keep far away from those things.
verses.discussion_question: If God hates something, does that mean He is being unkind? Or does it mean He cares deeply about what is right?
verses.family_discussion: How does knowing what God hates help us understand what God loves? What does this list tell us about His character?
verses.do_it_today: Read this verse again tonight as a family. Ask: why does God hate these things?
verses.illustration_prompt: An open scroll listing seven items glowing gently in warm light, with a reverent child reading carefully, watercolour style

verses.kjv: A proud look, a lying tongue, and hands that shed innocent blood,
verses.little_bible: God hates a proud, 'I-am-better-than-you' look, a mouth that tells lies, and hands that hurt innocent people.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: God hates pride, lying, and hurting the innocent.
verses.meaning: Pride, falsehood, and violence against the innocent are the first three items on God's list — all of them strike at the dignity and truth He created.
verses.memory_phrase: God loves humility, truth, and kindness.
verses.prayer: God, help me be humble, honest, and gentle — the opposite of what You hate.
verses.discussion_question: What does a proud look feel like when someone does it to you? How does it feel to be lied to?
verses.family_discussion: How can we build a home where humility, honesty, and kindness are normal? What would change if we lived like that every day?
verses.do_it_today: Do one humble thing today — let someone else go first, give credit to someone else, or say sorry for something.
verses.illustration_prompt: Three small scenes: a proud face reflected in a cracked mirror, a tongue with a shadow, and open caring hands — all in gentle watercolour

verses.kjv: An heart that deviseth wicked imaginations, feet that be swift in running to mischief,
verses.little_bible: God hates a heart that plans evil things, and feet that run quickly to do something wrong.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: God hates planning evil and rushing into trouble.
verses.meaning: God is concerned not just with actions but with the intentions of the heart. Planning evil and eagerly pursuing mischief are both abominations.
verses.memory_phrase: Think good thoughts — plan good things.
verses.prayer: God, guard my heart from thinking up bad plans. Help my feet run toward good things.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever thought about doing something wrong before you did it? What helped you choose not to?
verses.family_discussion: How does guarding our thoughts help us guard our actions? What can we fill our minds with that is good?
verses.do_it_today: Fill your mind with something good today — a Bible verse, a kind thought, a beautiful song.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child's heart glowing with golden light pushing out dark thoughts, while their feet walk toward a sunlit path, warm watercolour style

verses.kjv: A false witness that speaketh lies, and he that soweth discord among brethren.
verses.little_bible: God hates someone who tells lies about others, and someone who keeps starting fights between friends and family.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: God hates lying about others and starting fights.
verses.meaning: False witness destroys the innocent; sowing discord tears apart what God designed to be united. Both are deep violations of love and justice.
verses.memory_phrase: Speak truth. Make peace.
verses.prayer: God, help me always tell the truth about others and work to make peace, not trouble.
verses.discussion_question: How does a lie about someone else hurt that person — even if they never find out?
verses.family_discussion: How do we protect our family and friendships from false reports and gossip that cause division?
verses.do_it_today: If you hear something negative about someone today, choose not to pass it on. Instead, say something kind.
verses.illustration_prompt: Two children being hugged back together after a misunderstanding, with a bridge of golden light connecting them, watercolour style

verses.kjv: My son, keep thy father's commandment, and forsake not the law of thy mother:
verses.little_bible: My child, hold tight to what your father has taught you, and never let go of your mother's instructions.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Hold on to what Mum and Dad teach you.
verses.meaning: Both parents are presented as equal sources of God-given wisdom. Honouring their teaching is a sacred and practical act.
verses.memory_phrase: Hold on to what your parents teach.
verses.prayer: God, help me listen to and remember what my parents teach me. Thank You for giving them to me.
verses.discussion_question: What is the best thing your mum or dad has ever taught you?
verses.family_discussion: Parents: what is the one most important lesson you hope your child carries through life? Share it now.
verses.do_it_today: Tell a parent: 'Thank you for teaching me.' Name one specific thing they have taught you.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child walking between a mum and dad, each holding one hand, warm golden path ahead, love and safety in the colours, watercolour style

verses.kjv: Bind them continually upon thine heart, and tie them about thy neck.
verses.little_bible: Keep your parents' teaching so close that it is like something tied right around your heart and neck — always with you.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Keep their teaching always close to you.
verses.meaning: The image of binding wisdom to the heart and neck means internalising it completely — making it a permanent, visible part of who we are.
verses.memory_phrase: God's wisdom is always with me.
verses.prayer: God, help Your Word and my parents' teaching stay with me every single day.
verses.discussion_question: How do you carry something you want to remember everywhere with you? How can you do that with good teaching?
verses.family_discussion: What are ways our family keeps God's Word close — in our home, in our conversations, in our daily habits?
verses.do_it_today: Write one piece of good teaching on a small card and put it somewhere you will see it all day.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child with a glowing golden ribbon tied gently around their heart, little Bible verses sparkling along it, warm peaceful tones, watercolour style

verses.kjv: When thou goest, it shall lead thee; when thou sleepest, it shall keep thee; and when thou awakest, it shall talk with thee.
verses.little_bible: When you walk, wisdom will guide your steps. When you sleep, it will keep you safe. When you wake up, it will be there to talk with you!
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Wisdom guides me all day and keeps me safe at night.
verses.meaning: God's Word is a 24-hour companion — guiding us through the day, protecting us at night, and greeting us in the morning.
verses.memory_phrase: Wisdom guides me day and night.
verses.prayer: God, let Your wisdom be with me when I walk, when I sleep, and when I wake up.
verses.discussion_question: What does it feel like to know that God's wisdom is with you all day and all night?
verses.family_discussion: How can we make Scripture and wise teaching a natural part of morning, evening, and walking-around conversations in our home?
verses.do_it_today: Choose a Bible verse to think about when you wake up tomorrow morning. Read it tonight so it is ready.
verses.illustration_prompt: Three small scenes: a child walking in morning light with a glowing path, sleeping peacefully at night with a golden glow around them, and waking up smiling to an open Bible, watercolour triptych

verses.kjv: For the commandment is a lamp; and the law is light; and reproofs of instruction are the way of life:
verses.little_bible: God's commandments are like a lamp shining in the dark. His teaching is light — and even correction is a path that leads to life.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: God's Word is a lamp lighting my way.
verses.meaning: The famous metaphor of lamp and light speaks to the practical, moment-by-moment guidance that Scripture provides. Even reproof — correction — is life-giving when it comes from God.
verses.memory_phrase: God's Word is a lamp for my path.
verses.prayer: God, thank You that Your Word lights up my path. Help me follow where it leads.
verses.discussion_question: What does a lamp do in a dark room? How is God's Word like that for our lives?
verses.family_discussion: How does the Bible help us see things we could not see on our own — in our choices, our relationships, our future?
verses.do_it_today: Read one verse of the Bible as a family tonight and ask: what does this verse light up for us?
verses.illustration_prompt: A child holding a small glowing lantern walking through a gentle twilight forest, the path clearly lit ahead, warm and safe, watercolour style

verses.kjv: To keep thee from the evil woman, from the flattery of the tongue of a strange woman.
verses.little_bible: God's wisdom keeps you safe from people who use sweet, flattering words to lead you the wrong way.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: God's wisdom keeps you safe from tricky words.
verses.meaning: Wisdom serves as a guardian, helping us recognise when flattery is being used to manipulate us into wrong choices.
verses.memory_phrase: Wisdom keeps me from tricky words.
verses.prayer: God, help me recognise flattery and stay close to what is really true and good.
verses.discussion_question: What is the difference between a true compliment and flattery that is trying to get you to do something wrong?
verses.family_discussion: How do we help our children recognise when someone's kind words are actually pulling them away from what is right?
verses.do_it_today: Think of someone who always tells you the truth, even when it is not what you want to hear. Thank them today.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child protected by a gentle glowing shield as swirling deceptive words float past them harmlessly, warm and secure watercolour style

verses.kjv: Lust not after her beauty in thine heart; neither let her take thee with her eyelids.
verses.little_bible: Do not let something that looks beautiful on the outside pull your heart away from what is right. Some things that look attractive can lead you into trouble.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Do not let something beautiful tempt you to do wrong.
verses.meaning: Desire begins in the heart. What we allow ourselves to dwell on shapes where we go. Wisdom teaches us to guard our inner life.
verses.memory_phrase: Guard your heart from wrong desires.
verses.prayer: God, help me guard my heart so that beautiful things do not lead me away from what is right.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever wanted something so much that it made you think about doing something you should not? What happened?
verses.family_discussion: How do we talk about desire and self-control in a way that is honest and helpful in our family?
verses.do_it_today: Ask God to help you want the right things today — make it a morning prayer.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child looking at something shiny and appealing, but with a gentle hand on their shoulder guiding them to look away toward a brighter, truer light, watercolour style

verses.kjv: For by means of a whorish woman a man is brought to a piece of bread: and the adulteress will hunt for the precious life.
verses.little_bible: Wrong choices about friendships and relationships can cost everything — they can take away all that is precious and good in a person's life.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Wrong choices can cost us everything that is precious.
verses.meaning: Sin always costs more than we expect. What seems like a small wrong choice can lead to losing what is most valuable in life.
verses.memory_phrase: Wrong choices cost more than we think.
verses.prayer: God, help me see the real cost of wrong choices so I stay close to what is right.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think people sometimes trade something very precious for something that is not worth it?
verses.family_discussion: How do we help our family understand that every choice has a cost — and the cost of sin is always higher than it looks?
verses.do_it_today: Ask yourself: is there a choice in front of me that looks easy but might cost something precious? Talk to a parent about it.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child holding a precious glowing treasure safely, being warned by a wise figure about a shadowy path that would cost them what they hold dear, watercolour style

verses.kjv: Can a man take fire in his bosom, and his clothes not be burned?
verses.little_bible: Can you hold fire against your clothes without getting burned? Of course not! Some things always hurt if you get too close to them.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Hold fire and you will get burned — always.
verses.meaning: The illustration is vivid and universal: certain sins cannot be flirted with. Contact with them always brings damage.
verses.memory_phrase: Some things always burn if you touch them.
verses.prayer: God, help me stay far away from the things that will always hurt me.
verses.discussion_question: What are some things in life that are dangerous no matter how careful you are?
verses.family_discussion: How do we teach our children the difference between healthy caution and unhealthy fear — and how to stay safe without being fearful?
verses.do_it_today: Think of one thing you know is dangerous to get too close to. Make a plan to stay away from it.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child's hand pulling back from a flame — not burned, but warned — with a gentle knowing expression on their face, warm watercolour tones

verses.kjv: Can one go upon hot coals, and his feet not be burned?
verses.little_bible: Can someone walk across hot coals without burning their feet? No! Some things hurt everyone who touches them, no matter what.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Walk on hot coals and you will get burned.
verses.meaning: The second vivid image reinforces the point: there are no exceptions. Sin does not show favourites — it burns everyone who pursues it.
verses.memory_phrase: Sin burns — no exceptions.
verses.prayer: God, remind me that no one is special enough to escape the consequences of wrong choices.
verses.discussion_question: Why do people sometimes think rules about right and wrong do not apply to them?
verses.family_discussion: How do we help our children understand that God's boundaries exist to protect, not to restrict?
verses.do_it_today: Read Proverbs 6:27-28 together as a family and talk about what the fire and coals pictures really mean.
verses.illustration_prompt: A pair of small feet lifted up quickly from glowing red coals — a vivid, startled lesson about consequences — warm cautionary watercolour

verses.kjv: So he that goeth in to his neighbour's wife; whosoever toucheth her shall not be innocent.
verses.little_bible: In the same way, taking what belongs to someone else always brings harm. There is no way to do something wrong and come away unharmed.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Taking what is not yours always causes harm.
verses.meaning: The principle extends beyond the specific example: violating what belongs to another person brings unavoidable guilt and harm.
verses.memory_phrase: Respect what belongs to others.
verses.prayer: God, help me respect what belongs to other people and never take what is not mine.
verses.discussion_question: Why is it important to respect what belongs to other people?
verses.family_discussion: How do we cultivate respect for other people's belongings, relationships, and dignity in our home?
verses.do_it_today: Return something you have borrowed, ask before using something that is not yours, or thank someone for sharing with you.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child gently placing back a toy that belongs to another child, with a peaceful smile — choosing to respect rather than take, watercolour style

verses.kjv: Men do not despise a thief, if he steal to satisfy his soul when he is hungry;
verses.little_bible: People feel sorry for someone who steals because they are starving — they understand how desperate hunger can be.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: People feel sorry for a hungry thief.
verses.meaning: The text acknowledges human compassion — even when someone does wrong out of desperate need, people understand. But understanding does not erase the consequences.
verses.memory_phrase: We can understand and still know it is wrong.
verses.prayer: God, give me a heart that is both compassionate and wise — to understand people and still choose what is right.
verses.discussion_question: Can something be both understandable and still wrong? How?
verses.family_discussion: How do we show compassion to people who are desperate while still upholding what is true and right?
verses.do_it_today: Do something kind for someone who is in need today — give food, time, or encouragement.
verses.illustration_prompt: A compassionate child handing a piece of bread to a hungry person on the street, with gentle warmth and dignity in the scene, watercolour style

verses.kjv: But if he be found, he shall restore sevenfold; he shall give all the substance of his house.
verses.little_bible: But if he is caught, he must give back much more than he took — even everything he has. Wrong choices always cost more than we planned.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: A thief caught must pay back much more.
verses.meaning: Even when circumstances seem to excuse wrong behaviour, consequences remain. Restitution and responsibility are part of God's justice.
verses.memory_phrase: Wrong choices always cost more than we plan.
verses.prayer: God, help me understand that every wrong choice has a cost. Help me choose right from the start.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think the punishment is so much greater than the crime in this story?
verses.family_discussion: How does our family practise making restitution — doing more than just saying sorry when we have caused harm?
verses.do_it_today: Think of someone you have wronged. What would real restoration look like? Take one step toward it.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child carefully handing back an overflowing basket of things to make things right, looking both sorry and determined, warm watercolour scene

verses.kjv: But whoso committeth adultery with a woman lacketh understanding: he that doeth it destroyeth his own soul.
verses.little_bible: Someone who makes wrong choices about relationships is not thinking clearly — they are harming themselves most of all.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Wrong choices about relationships hurt us most.
verses.meaning: Sin is ultimately self-destructive. The person who violates God's design for relationships harms their own soul above all else.
verses.memory_phrase: Wrong choices always hurt the one who makes them.
verses.prayer: God, help me make choices that protect my soul, not ones that damage it.
verses.discussion_question: How can a wrong choice hurt the person who makes it the most?
verses.family_discussion: How do we help our children understand that God's boundaries for relationships are there to protect them, not to limit them?
verses.do_it_today: Ask God today: 'Show me if there are any choices I am making that are secretly hurting me.'
verses.illustration_prompt: A figure walking away from a bright path into shadow, looking over their shoulder at the light they are leaving, gentle warning in the image, watercolour style

verses.kjv: A wound and dishonour shall he get; and his reproach shall not be wiped away.
verses.little_bible: He will end up hurt and ashamed — and the damage to his reputation will be very hard to repair.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Wrong choices leave wounds that are hard to heal.
verses.meaning: The consequences of moral failure are lasting. Shame and broken reputation are real wounds that do not heal quickly.
verses.memory_phrase: Some wounds are very hard to heal.
verses.prayer: God, help me choose things today that I will not be ashamed of tomorrow.
verses.discussion_question: Why is a broken reputation so hard to rebuild?
verses.family_discussion: How do we build a reputation that is worth having? What daily choices go into it?
verses.do_it_today: Do something today that you will still be proud of tomorrow. Choose integrity.
verses.illustration_prompt: A child polishing a shield that has a dent in it — a picture of working hard to repair what was damaged, hopeful watercolour style

verses.kjv: For jealousy is the rage of a man: therefore he will not spare in the day of vengeance.
verses.little_bible: Jealousy can make a person very, very angry — and an angry, jealous person can be very hard to deal with.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jealousy turns into a powerful, dangerous anger.
verses.meaning: Jealousy is not a minor emotion — it produces fierce, consuming anger that does not easily relent. This is one of the devastating consequences of betrayal.
verses.memory_phrase: Jealousy leads to dangerous anger.
verses.prayer: God, protect me from jealousy. Help me rejoice in what others have instead of being envious.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever felt jealous of someone? What did that feel like in your body?
verses.family_discussion: How do we deal with jealousy in a healthy way — talking about it, praying about it — before it grows into anger?
verses.do_it_today: Is there someone you are feeling jealous of? Pray for them today and ask God to bless them.
verses.illustration_prompt: A stormy sky inside a chest representing a jealous heart, with a small crack of gentle light showing the way to peace, watercolour style

verses.kjv: He will not regard any ransom; neither will he rest content, though thou givest many gifts.
verses.little_bible: No amount of gifts or payment can make some things right. When trust is broken deeply, it cannot always be bought back.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Some wrongs cannot be fixed with gifts.
verses.meaning: There are consequences that money cannot undo. This truth reinforces the gravity of sin — especially sins that betray trust and relationship.
verses.memory_phrase: Some wrongs go beyond what money can fix.
verses.prayer: God, help me understand that trust is precious and must be protected — not just patched up with gifts.
verses.discussion_question: Is there a difference between saying sorry and truly making things right? What is it?
verses.family_discussion: How does our family build deep trust? What do we do when that trust is broken?
verses.do_it_today: Think about someone you trust deeply. Do something today to show them you treasure that trust.
verses.illustration_prompt: A pile of gifts sitting unopened beside a broken friendship — a child looking sad, realising some things cannot be fixed with things, watercolour style
